message:
Add SCP pack for remote copy protection

SCP pack (remote.scp):
- 8 destructive patterns for system path overwrites (/etc, /var, /usr, /boot, /bin, /lib)
- 5 safe patterns: help, downloads to cwd, copies to home/tmp
- Protects against accidental overwrites to critical system directories

Closes: git_safety_guard-re7

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.5","title":"Eliminate duplicate normalization/classification","description":"Refactor evaluator flow to avoid repeated normalize + classify passes.","notes":"Currently we normalize/classify in quick-reject and later re-normalize for pack matching. Reuse the normalized/sanitized view when safe.

Invariant: if sanitize_for_pattern_matching returns Owned, the keyword gate must re-run on the sanitized form (current behavior). After that, only normalize once and reuse for pack checks.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.6","title":"Safe-pattern RegexSet fast path","description":"Use RegexSet to skip per-pattern safe checks when no safe pattern can match.","notes":"Approach: build a RegexSet for linear-safe patterns per pack (or per tier). If set misses, skip safe pattern checks. If set hits, fall back to ordered evaluation to preserve first-match semantics.

Isomorphism: set is only used as a negative filter; any potential match must still be validated by the original ordered logic.

Testing/Validation:

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.9","title":"Advanced: deterministic rm parser (regex replacement)","description":"Consider replacing rm regex families with a deterministic parser for flags + paths.","notes":"Goal: reduce regex backtracking risk and simplify reasoning. Parser must exactly implement current allow/block rules (temp-dir allowlist, root/home block, flag ordering, long/short flags).

This is only acceptable if behavior is provably identical against the golden corpus.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-kya","title":"[PACK] monitoring.pagerduty - PagerDuty Pack","description":"# PagerDuty Monitoring Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for PagerDuty CLI and API operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### pd CLI
- `pd service delete`
- `pd schedule delete`
- `pd escalation-policy delete`
- `pd user delete`
- `pd team delete`

### API Operations
- `curl -X DELETE api.pagerduty.com/.../services/`
- `curl -X DELETE api.pagerduty.com/.../schedules/`

## Safe Patterns
- `pd service list`, `pd service get`
- `pd schedule list`, `pd schedule get`
- `pd incident list`
- API GET requests

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: monitoring.* (git_safety_guard-adw)
- Keywords: pd
- Note: Service deletion breaks incident routing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Service deletion blocked
- [ ] Schedule deletion blocked
- [ ] Escalation policy deletion blocked
- [ ] Tests cover common PagerDuty operations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:34:16.581669889-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T22:23:07.692800176-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T22:23:07.692800176-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-kya","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:37.388021854-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-kya","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:37.429946375-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-kya","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:37.473830083-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-kya","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:37.517133709-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-kya","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:37.560984075-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-kya","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:37.605839431-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-kya","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:10.996855202-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-kya","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.042217989-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-kyi","title":"[PACK] messaging.kafka - Apache Kafka Pack","description":"# Apache Kafka Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Kafka CLI tools and operations.

## Destructive Patterns
- `kafka-topics --delete` / `kafka-topics.sh --delete`
- `kafka-consumer-groups --delete`
- `kafka-consumer-groups --reset-offsets`
- `kafka-configs --alter --delete-config`
- `kafka-acls --remove`
- `rpk topic delete` (Redpanda CLI, Kafka-compatible)

## Safe Patterns
- `--describe`, `--list`
- `kafka-topics --list`
- `kafka-consumer-groups --describe`
- `kafka-configs --describe`
- `kafka-console-consumer`, `kafka-console-producer`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: messaging.* (git_safety_guard-me0)
- Keywords: kafka-topics, kafka-consumer-groups, kafka-configs, kafka-acls, rpk
- Note: Both .sh scripts and bare commands used

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Topic deletion blocked
- [ ] Consumer group operations handled
- [ ] Offset reset blocked
- [ ] Redpanda CLI covered

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-qr6","title":"[PACK] cicd.gitlab_ci - GitLab CI Pack","description":"# GitLab CI Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for GitLab CI/CD variables and pipeline operations.

## Destructive Patterns
- `glab variable delete` - Removes CI variables
- `glab ci delete` - Deletes pipeline artifacts
- `glab api -X DELETE .../variables/`
- `gitlab-runner unregister` - Unregisters runners

## Safe Patterns
- `glab variable list`
- `glab ci list`, `glab ci view`
- `glab ci status`
- `gitlab-runner list`, `gitlab-runner status`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: cicd.* (git_safety_guard-0v8)
- Keywords: glab, gitlab-runner
- Overlaps with platform.gitlab for broader GitLab operations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Variable deletion blocked
- [ ] Runner unregistration blocked
- [ ] Pipeline operations handled
- [ ] Tests cover common GitLab CI workflows","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:31:27.39470727-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T17:42:06.751253341-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T17:42:06.751253341-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented GitLab CI pack, registered in cicd module + registry, added tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qr6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.056604561-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qr6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.096505335-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qr6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.138085769-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qr6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.178316444-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qr6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.222956114-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qr6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.261737141-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qr6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:09.765534832-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qr6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:09.811421627-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","title":"[TASK] Performance regression testing for packs","description":"# Performance Regression Testing for Packs

## Scope
Ensure adding new packs doesn't degrade dcg performance.

## Deliverables

### 1. Performance Benchmark Suite
Create `benches/pack_performance.rs`:
```rust
use criterion::{criterion_group, Criterion};

fn bench_single_pack_evaluation(c: &mut Criterion) {
    // Benchmark each pack individually
}

fn bench_all_packs_evaluation(c: &mut Criterion) {
    // Benchmark with all packs enabled
}

fn bench_pattern_matching(c: &mut Criterion) {
    // Benchmark regex pattern matching specifically
}
```

### 2. Performance Budgets
Define maximum acceptable times:
- Single pack evaluation: < 500µs
- All packs evaluation: < 5ms
- Regex pattern match: < 100µs per pattern
- Total hook execution: < 10ms

### 3. CI Performance Tracking
- Run benchmarks on every PR
- Compare against baseline
- Fail PR if regression > 10%
- Track performance over time (grafana/dashboard)

### 4. Performance Logging
When DEBUG enabled:
- Log time per pack
- Log time per pattern
- Identify slow patterns
- Suggest optimizations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Benchmark suite covers all performance-critical paths
- [ ] Budgets defined and enforced in CI
- [ ] Performance tracked over time
- [ ] Slow patterns flagged for optimization","notes":"Context (perf reality check):
- Current process-per-command baseline (release) is ~173–179ms p50 for normal hook invocations because pack registry initialization compiles regexes.
- Criterion benches (in-process) are still useful, but they do NOT capture process startup + per-invocation regex compilation cost.

Plan adjustments:
- Treat `git_safety_guard-64dc` (lazy registry + lazy compilation) as the prerequisite that makes pack performance budgets meaningful.
- After startup costs are eliminated, criterion benches can focus on steady-state evaluation cost (sanitize/normalize/pack matching).
- Add a process-level harness (JSON baseline + diff) via `git_safety_guard-ksk.1.3` so CI can gate on p95/p99 for the allow path.

Budget note:
- The "Total hook execution < 10ms" budget is a good target but should be enforced only after the startup refactor lands, otherwise it will fail constantly and become noise.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:34:15.054793749-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T16:39:21.26259381-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T16:39:21.26259381-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: Updated heredoc_perf.rs to include cicd and safe.cleanup packs in worst-case scenarios, verified performance remains within budgets.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-64dc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:54:17.803032297-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:54:22.89206455-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-rbst","title":"Implement robustness and edge case handling for heredoc parsing","description":"# Robustness and Edge Case Handling for Heredoc/Inline Script Analysis

## Objective

Ensure heredoc detection + extraction + AST matching are *robust by construction*:

- never panic
- never hang
- never allocate unbounded memory
- fail-open on malformed input/timeouts (hook safety)

This task focuses on **edge cases** and **hardening** (the stuff that only shows up in the wild).

## Background

Heredoc and inline-script analysis is the highest-risk path for stability because inputs are:

- multiline
- potentially huge
- often malformed (especially from agents)
- can include confusing quoting/indentation variants

A single crash/hang in this path makes users disable dcg.

## Hardening requirements (v1)

### 1) Size limits (must be enforced)

- enforce a max extracted body size and max line count
- limits must be configurable (see global limit work) and *never* exceed sane defaults

Behavior:

- if limits are exceeded: **skip deep analysis** and fail-open
- record a structured log/event when this happens (so it is debuggable)

### 2) Deadline / timeout protection (must be enforced)

- tier 2 extraction must be bounded
- tier 3 AST parsing/matching must be bounded
- if using external processes (ast-grep), they must have a hard timeout + kill

Behavior:

- on timeout: skip deep analysis, fail-open, log `skipped_due_to_budget`

### 3) Malformed heredoc handling (must be graceful)

Handle without panic:

- missing delimiter
- unterminated heredoc
- invalid delimiter tokens
- invalid indentation stripping cases (`<<-`)
- multiple heredocs in a single command

### 4) Non-UTF8 / binary-ish content (must be safe)

- detect binary-like payloads (null bytes, high non-printable ratio)
- do not feed binary-ish input into AST parsing

Behavior:

- skip analysis and fail-open; log `skipped_binary_content`

### 5) Multiple heredocs / nested triggers (bounded)

- support multiple heredocs per command up to a small cap
- if nested heredoc triggers appear inside a body:
  - treat as **low-confidence** signal initially (WARN/log), not auto-DENY
  - avoid recursion in v1; recursion can be a separate follow-up if needed

## Observability requirements

When deep analysis is skipped, the system must surface why (in trace/log):

- exceeded size limits
- deadline/budget exceeded
- parse error / malformed input
- binary-ish content detected

## Acceptance criteria

- No panics for arbitrary inputs (validated via fuzz/proptest targets).
- Extraction and analysis never exceed configured limits/budgets.
- Skips are visible in `dcg explain` and structured logs.
- Multiple heredocs are handled up to the configured cap.

## Tests

- Unit tests:
  - oversized heredoc → skip + fail-open + trace/log marker
  - unterminated heredoc → skip + fail-open
  - binary-ish heredoc → skip + fail-open
  - multiple heredocs parsed up to cap

- Fuzzing:
  - heredoc extraction fuzz target must find no panics

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-99e.10` global size limits (single source of truth)
- `git_safety_guard-99e.14` evaluation time budget/deadline semantics
- `git_safety_guard-y4j` extraction implementation
- `git_safety_guard-tlog` logging/trace integration for heredoc path
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyMill","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:00:00-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T02:00:11.048164731-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T02:00:11.048164731-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented: size limits, binary detection, malformed input handling, multiple heredoc bounds, SkipReason observability. All 257 tests pass. Timeout enforcement deferred until Tier 3 AST matching. Fuzz targets tracked separately.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rbst","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-2ta","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:46:02.228572246-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rbst","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-y4j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:47:10.592550683-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rbst","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:50:34.385023947-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-re7","title":"[PACK] remote.scp - SCP Pack","description":"# SCP Remote Copy Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for SCP operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### Overwrite Operations
- Copying to critical system paths
- Overwriting without backup
- Wrong direction copy (source/dest swapped)

### Recursive Operations
- `scp -r` to sensitive directories

## Safe Patterns
- `scp file user@host:path` (standard copy)
- `scp user@host:file .` (download)
- Copying to user directories

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: remote.* (git_safety_guard-g5o)
- Keywords: scp
- Note: scp is being deprecated in favor of sftp/rsync

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for critical path overwrites
- [ ] Unit tests for recursive operations
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/remote_scp.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Critical path overwrites detected
- [ ] Standard file copies allowed","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:11:14.428556049-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T03:02:53.309847524-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-re7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.859292589-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-re7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.904909447-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-re7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.950848202-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-re7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.998184937-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-re7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.044047828-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-re7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.089802537-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-re7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.272188302-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-re7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.318489576-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","title":"[PACK] storage.minio - MinIO Pack","description":"# MinIO Object Storage Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for MinIO (S3-compatible) operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### mc (MinIO Client)
- `mc rb` - Remove bucket
- `mc rb --force` - Force remove
- Remove with recursive flag
- `mc admin bucket delete`

## Safe Patterns
- `mc ls`
- `mc cat`
- `mc stat`
- `mc cp` (copy)
- `mc diff`
- `mc admin info`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: storage.* (git_safety_guard-skt)
- Keywords: mc (MinIO client)
- Note: S3-compatible, similar patterns to aws s3

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for bucket removal
- [ ] Unit tests for recursive deletion
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/storage_minio.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bucket removal blocked
- [ ] Recursive deletion blocked
- [ ] List/copy operations allowed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:40.626641176-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:40.626641176-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.487828632-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.530312907-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.573780863-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.6171008-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.662252443-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.707883469-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.739543506-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.787195064-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-rl8","title":"Enforce coverage thresholds in CI (llvm-cov)","description":"## Objective

Turn coverage reporting from informational to **enforced**, with documented thresholds and failure behavior.

## Scope

- Use `cargo llvm-cov` output already produced in CI.
- Define initial minimum thresholds (global + key modules).
- Fail CI when thresholds are not met.

## Notes

- Thresholds should be set **after** baseline audit (see dependencies).
- Keep thresholds modest at first, with a follow-up plan to ratchet.

## Acceptance criteria

- CI fails when coverage drops below defined thresholds.
- Coverage summary still attached as artifact / job summary.
- Thresholds documented in the repo (doc or CI comments).

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-rm2n","title":"[TASK] User experience for pack discovery and feedback","description":"# User Experience for Pack Discovery and Feedback

## Scope
Ensure users can easily discover, configure, and provide feedback on packs.

## Deliverables

### 1. Pack Discovery CLI Commands
Implement new dcg commands:
```bash
# List all available packs
dcg packs list

# Show details about a specific pack
dcg packs info secrets.vault

# Show which packs would match a command
dcg explain 'vault secrets disable foo'

# Show packs enabled in current config
dcg packs enabled
```

### 2. Pack Configuration UX
Make configuration intuitive:
```toml
# .dcg.toml example with clear documentation
[packs]
# Enable all Tier 1 packs (critical security)
tier1 = true

# Selectively enable Tier 2 packs
enable = ["dns.cloudflare", "monitoring.datadog"]

# Disable specific packs if needed  
disable = ["backup.rclone"]  # We use custom backup scripts
```

### 3. Helpful Error Messages
When a command is blocked:
```
⚠️  BLOCKED: vault secrets disable production-secrets

Pack: secrets.vault
Reason: 'vault secrets disable' permanently disables a secrets engine,
        potentially causing data loss for all secrets stored there.

This looks intentional? You can:
  1. Add to allowlist: dcg allow 'vault secrets disable production-secrets'
  2. Run with bypass: DCG_BYPASS=1 vault secrets disable production-secrets
  3. Report false positive: https://github.com/.../issues/new?template=false_positive

Docs: https://dcg.dev/packs/secrets/vault
```

### 4. Feedback Collection
- GitHub issue templates for:
  - False positive reports
  - False negative reports  
  - New pack requests
- Telemetry (opt-in) for pattern match statistics
- User survey mechanism

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pack list/info commands implemented
- [ ] Configuration is well-documented
- [ ] Error messages are helpful and actionable
- [ ] Feedback channels are clear and accessible","notes":"Testing/Validation:
- Unit tests for CLI output shape, sorting, and redaction.
- E2E CLI scenarios (list/detail/configure/feedback) using the shared harness with verbose logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:35:00.15029369-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T17:03:12.642746777-05:00"}
